title: Carrot cake with cream cheese frosting
subtitle: Spiced carrot cake baked in rounds, frosted with cream cheese
excerpt: A moist, spiced carrot cake made with oil rather than butter, baked in two 20 cm layers and frosted with a sharp cream cheese icing. Keeps well for three days. Makes one two-layer cake.
sourceNotes: Carrot cake in its current oil-based, cream-cheese-frosted form is a twentieth-century American cake that became popular in Britain from the 1970s. The use of raw grated carrot in a spiced oil cake is documented in American household baking guides from the mid-twentieth century. No single public-domain text records this combination. The recipe draws on the widely reproduced ratio of oil, egg, and grated carrot that appears across American baking publications from that period.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted sponge layers. Wrap individually. Frost from thawed.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The frosted cake keeps refrigerated for up to 3 days. Remove from the fridge 30 minutes before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 190°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: An icing made from cream cheese, icing sugar, and sometimes a small amount of butter. It is sharper and less sweet than buttercream, which makes it the natural pairing for sweet, spiced cakes. The cream cheese must be cold — warm cream cheese frosting is loose and will not hold. Do not overbeat once the cream cheese is incorporated or it will thin.

content.text: The oil-based method produces a carrot cake that stays moist for days where a butter cake would dry out. Use a neutral oil — sunflower or light rapeseed. Walnuts are the classic addition; leave them out if the cake is going anywhere near nut allergies. The carrots should be freshly grated and not squeezed dry.
Make the batter and bake — 28–32 minutes

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease and line two 20 cm round tins. Whisk together the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{mixed-spice}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. In a separate bowl, whisk the {{eggs}}, {{vegetable-oil}}, and {{whole-milk}}.

Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and stir until just combined. Fold in the {{carrot}} and the {{walnuts}} if using. Divide between the tins and bake for 28–32 minutes, until well-risen and a skewer inserted into the centre comes out clean. Cool in the tins for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack and cool completely before frosting.

Beat the softened {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth. Add the cold {{cream-cheese}} and beat briefly until just combined. Add the sifted {{icing-sugar}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and mix on low speed until smooth. Do not overbeat. The
should be thick and hold its shape. Spread half over the top of one sponge, place the second sponge on top, and spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
